messages.content: We are revisiting our background job/task queue. What did we decide for it, and why — and which alternatives did we rule out? Find the decision record in my ADR log.

ADR-01 (Accepted 2026-02-03) Adopt pnpm workspaces over npm: decided to adopt it | why: one lockfile and faster CI across packages | rejected: Yarn (slower cold installs), Nx (too heavy for our size)
ADR-02 (Accepted 2026-03-04) Validate API boundaries with Zod: decided to adopt it | why: runtime validation plus inferred types from one schema | rejected: io-ts (worse ergonomics), hand-written guards (drift from types)
ADR-03 (Accepted 2026-04-05) Standardize services on Hono: decided to adopt it | why: tiny, edge-ready, OpenAPI-friendly | rejected: Express (heavier, callback style), Fastify (more plugins to vet)
ADR-04 (Accepted 2026-05-06) Encrypt stored content with AES-256-GCM: decided to adopt it | why: the content-privacy invariant, per-tenant keys | rejected: plain hashing (not reversible), KMS-only (per-call latency)
ADR-05 (Accepted 2026-02-07) Pin provider model ids in config: decided to adopt it | why: reproducible routing and billing | rejected: floating "latest" aliases (silent behaviour drift)
ADR-06 (Accepted 2026-03-08) Publish docs on Docusaurus: decided to adopt it | why: MDX, versioning, and a themeable design system | rejected: Nextra (less mature theming), a hand-rolled site (maintenance)
ADR-07 (Accepted 2026-04-09) Keep Postgres as the system of record: decided to adopt it | why: transactional and well understood by the team | rejected: Mongo (schema drift), DynamoDB (awkward ad-hoc reads)
ADR-08 (Accepted 2026-05-10) Ship a single root docker-compose: decided to adopt it | why: one command brings the whole stack up | rejected: per-service compose files (drift), a k8s-only dev loop (too slow)
ADR-09 (Accepted 2026-02-11) Attribute spend via an x-anyray-metadata header: decided to adopt it | why: content-free per-user and per-team attribution | rejected: parsing auth tokens (brittle), a sidecar (more moving parts)
ADR-031 (Accepted 2026-04-02) Background job/task queue: decided on Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY backed by a jobs table | why: one datastore, the enqueue is transactional with the business write, and no new ops surface to run | rejected: Redis Streams (another datastore to operate, weaker exactly-once for us), SQS (vendor lock-in and painful local dev) | consequences: revisit a dedicated broker only past very high throughput
ADR-11 (Accepted 2026-04-13) Gate the console behind one admin token: decided to adopt it | why: a simple operator surface for self-host | rejected: full RBAC (premature), per-route keys (sprawl)
ADR-12 (Accepted 2026-05-14) Fail open when the optimizer is slow or down: decided to adopt it | why: the gateway must never add latency risk | rejected: fail closed (blocks traffic), no timeout (tail-latency risk)
ADR-13 (Accepted 2026-02-15) Store secrets in a provider-keys bag, not env: decided to adopt it | why: rotate without a redeploy; works on AWS | rejected: instance-role auth (unsupported by the signer), dotenv files (leak risk)
ADR-14 (Accepted 2026-03-16) Use Langfuse as the observability engine: decided to adopt it | why: a mature tracing UI we can white-label | rejected: building tracing in-house (months of work), a SaaS-only tool (data residency)
ADR-15 (Accepted 2026-04-17) Write filenames in camelCase: decided to adopt it | why: match the existing tree and avoid churn | rejected: kebab-case (inconsistent), snake_case (non-idiomatic for TS)
ADR-16 (Accepted 2026-05-18) Default content mode to encrypted: decided to adopt it | why: private by default; plaintext is deploy-gated | rejected: plaintext default (privacy risk), off (loses traces)
ADR-17 (Accepted 2026-02-19) Adopt ESM across all packages: decided to adopt it | why: one module system and top-level await | rejected: CommonJS (legacy), a dual build (extra complexity)
ADR-18 (Accepted 2026-03-20) Meter every deployment through the Portal: decided to adopt it | why: usage visibility; the metering stays content-free | rejected: an optional air-gapped path (unmetered and unsupported)
ADR-19 (Accepted 2026-04-21) Tune a global upstream dispatcher timeout: decided to adopt it | why: kill the 300s default freeze on stalled sockets | rejected: no timeout (hangs), a per-request timeout (config sprawl)
